description: Ambiguous one-word reply ('Maybe.') with non-empty text. No det-* rule matches and no mock-classifier keyword pattern matches, so the mock classifier returns AMBIGUOUS at confidence 0.5. Section C's confidence threshold (0.60) then overrides to AMBIGUOUS with confidence_overridden=true. Distinguishes the confidence-override path from the operational empty-reply path (op-empty-reply, see fixture notes).

input.reply_subject: Re: Quick question about your outbound pipeline

evidence: mock classifier: no deterministic or keyword match found

expected.notes: 'Maybe.' has non-empty trimmed text (wordCount=1), so prefilter.is_empty_reply=false and Section A does not assign op-empty-reply. mockSemanticClassify returns AMBIGUOUS at confidence 0.5 (the 'wordCount > 0' branch, not the 0.3 empty-reply branch). Section C then sees category!==null and confidence(0.5) < 0.60, so it sets effectiveCategory=AMBIGUOUS (unchanged) and confidence_overridden=true with reason citing the 0.60 threshold. This is distinct from a genuinely empty reply, which would instead get deterministic_match=true, rule_id='op-empty-reply', confidence=1, confidence_overridden=false.
